question.text: If two call options on USDINR have the same strike but different expiry dates, the one with longer expiry will:

answer.text: Have a higher premium (everything else being equal)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Longer time to expiry = more uncertainty = higher time value = higher premium. A 2-month call costs more than a 1-month call (same strike), because there's more time for the underlying to move favorably. **The Trap:** Time value is always non-negative before expiry and increases with remaining time. **Cross-Exam Context:** Time-premium relationship is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This is why rolling positions to the next month costs money — you're buying more time value.
